summary.contentAssessment: The source is a self-contained didactic pathology module on granulomatous lung disease with a strong tutorial core, a stepwise diagnostic pathway, ADDIE lecture-style sections, and slide-viewer dependencies.
notes: No standalone local image files were found; image content is embedded through remote DZI slide sources and a single inline JPEG example.
The ADDIE sections are pedagogical lecture/meta-narrative content rather than app scaffolding.
The WSI viewer is a dependency for image-based learning but is not itself a content payload.

algorithms.reason: Implements the interactive decision-tree diagnostic pathway and compendium table.

lectures.reason: ADDIE lecture-style analysis content with embedded knowledge checks.
ADDIE design lecture content covering learning objectives and Bloom's levels.
ADDIE development narrative plus worked instructional examples.
ADDIE evaluation content with a board-style question and explanatory feedback.

images.reason: Contains an embedded WSI example and an image-backed case workflow.
Uses two WSI slide sources for visual discrimination challenges.
Uses an inline histology image URL as part of the worked case.
Image-viewer dependency for whole-slide imaging content.

tutorials.reason: Introduces the learning module and navigation to the didactic activities.
Reference-style quick aid comparing sarcoidosis and tuberculosis.
Guided case-based tutorial with clinical reasoning and reveal-answer flow.
Board-style tutorial question with interactive feedback.
Interactive tutorial workflow for generating and critiquing new cases.

dependencies.role: Root navigation and section composition for the module.
Navigation shell used by the learning module.
Shared presentation wrapper used across content sections.
Shared UI icon set used by educational views.
Viewer dependency required for slide-based content.

recommendation: Migrate the tutorial, lecture, and algorithm sections as structured content first, then normalize the image-linked case material separately. Do not expect local image assets; the module depends on remote WSI tile sources and one inline example image.
